content.full_text:
R,vi M LirrA
CIIAIMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
May 2,
1996
Honorable Robert Thompson
Justice Court of Appeal
(Retired)
6105 Camino De La Costa
La Jolla,
CA
92037
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1-96-122
Dear Justice Thompson:
You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
!
Because your inquiry is general in nature,
we will treat your
letter as a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
18329(c)
2/
QUESTION
You are a retired justice and were assigned to work on a
single case for the Second District Court of Appeal.
Although
your assignment extended beyond 30 days during 1995,
you actually
served less than 30 days during the year.
To determine if you are
required to file an annual statement of economic interests
(Form
721),
you asked if the period of service is determined by the
length of the assignment or the actual time served.
CONCLUSION
The filing obligation is based on actual time served.
You must file an annual statement of economic interests
(Form 721)
only if you served 30 days or more during the calendar year.
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329 (c) (3)
. )
2X i S-u
-i Si
fl
SHiili fl. (i ik\I-\ ‘)5X14
916 .25ct


Justice Thompson
Our File No.
1—96—122
Page Two
ANALYSIS
The Act requires public officials including judges to file a
statement each year disclosing their personal financial interests
in order that conflicts of interest may be avoided.
(Sections
81002(c),
87200, et seq.)
Furthermore, Regulation 18724 specifies
that retired judges who serve on assignment shall file annual
statements of economic interests for each calendar year in which
they serve 30 days or more.
Although a retired judge may be on assignment for more than
30 days, the actual time he or she serves determines whether a
statement of economic interests must be filed.
(Barton Advice
Letter,
No.
1—95—410.)
Therefore, since you actually served less
than 30 days during 1995,
you have no obligation to file a
statement of economic inteests for 1995.
Please call me at
(916)
322—5660 if you have additional
questions.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
